ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for prata bomb. FOODON_03315021 (flatbread food product) is the closest defensible ancestor. Condensed milk filling variant is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Flat bread) is the closest defensible code. No specific code exists for stuffed prata bomb with condensed milk in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for prata bomb or roti prata variants. SNOMED CT does not cover this regional dish at the required specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One prata bomb with condensed milk typically weighs approximately 150 g, representing a single serving as commonly sold at mamak stalls and hawker centres in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Prata Bomb (Condensed Milk) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Prata Bomb (Condensed Milk), CR ID F05020095. All seven reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are taken verbatim. Saturated fat is estimated from ingredient composition (margarine and condensed milk contribution) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for prata bomb with condensed milk. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the closest analogous food: white wheat flatbread / roti (GI ~70, University of Sydney GI Database). The condensed milk filling adds rapidly absorbed sugars, supporting a high-GI classification. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (70 × 74.43) / 100 ≈ 52.1, rounded to 52.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 484 mg/100 g combined with high sugar 13.3 g/100 g and low fibre 0.7 g/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid prata bomb with condensed milk: sodium at 484 mg/100 g (approximately 726 mg per 150 g serving) approaches one-third of the daily sodium limit, and the high sugar content (13.3 g/100 g with only 0.7 g fibre) independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance on dietary sugar and hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate 49.62 g/100 g with very low fibre 0.7 g/100 g and high sugar 13.3 g/100 g from condensed milk; estimated GI 70
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit prata bomb with condensed milk: the combination of high-GI refined flour (estimated GI 70), 13.3 g sugar/100 g from condensed milk, and negligible fibre (0.7 g/100 g) produces a very high glycemic load (~52 per serving), causing rapid and sustained postprandial glucose elevation; if consumed, limit to a quarter portion paired with a protein-rich accompaniment and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~7.8 g/100 g from margarine and condensed milk, trans fat ~0.3 g/100 g from margarine, and high sodium 484 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid prata bomb with condensed milk: the estimated saturated fat of approximately 7.8 g/100 g and trans fat of approximately 0.3 g/100 g from margarine raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis, while sodium at 484 mg/100 g and high added sugar from condensed milk further elevate cardiovascular risk; this food is not suitable for heart-healthy dietary patterns.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat ~0.3 g/100 g from margarine and estimated saturated fat ~7.8 g/100 g are pro-inflammatory; high sugar 13.3 g/100 g promotes inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid prata bomb with condensed milk: trans fat from margarine directly triggers pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways, estimated saturated fat exceeds the RA threshold without MUFA/PUFA offset, and high sugar (13.3 g/100 g) further promotes systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prata bomb with condensed milk is a popular Singaporean mamak hawker dish and a thicker, richer variant of the classic roti prata flatbread. It is made from a laminated refined wheat flour dough that is stretched, folded with generous amounts of margarine, and pan-fried on a flat griddle until the exterior is golden and crisp while the interior remains soft and layered. The 'bomb' designation refers to its domed, puffed shape achieved by trapping steam during cooking. Sweetened condensed milk is drizzled over or stuffed inside the prata, adding a rich, sweet, creamy flavour that contrasts with the slightly salty, buttery dough. The texture is simultaneously crispy on the outside and pillowy within, with a pronounced sweetness from the condensed milk. Nutritionally, prata bomb with condensed milk is energy-dense at 378 kcal/100 g, with significant fat (16.96 g/100 g, estimated saturated fat ~7.8 g/100 g from margarine and condensed milk), high carbohydrate (49.62 g/100 g), high sugar (13.3 g/100 g), and elevated sodium (484 mg/100 g). Dietary fibre is very low at 0.7 g/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 70 (high), and a typical 150 g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 52, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. It is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or late-night snack at mamak stalls and hawker centres across Singapore and Malaysia.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F05020095) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from ingredient composition (margarine and condensed milk) using USDA FoodData Central reference values for these ingredients, as HPB does not separately report these fractions for this entry. GI is an analogous estimate based on white wheat flatbread from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (7.8 g/100 g) is estimated from ingredient composition: margarine contributes approximately 20–25% saturated fat of its fat content, and condensed milk contributes approximately 60% saturated fat of its fat content; combined estimate is consistent with the total fat of 16.96 g/100 g. This value is not directly reported by HPB for this entry. 2. Trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) is estimated from margarine content using USDA FoodData Central reference values for partially hydrogenated vegetable fat spreads; HPB does not report trans fat for this entry. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient, fatty acid fractionation, and moisture data for this specific prata bomb preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of April 2026. 4. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white wheat flatbread (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for prata bomb with condensed milk has been published in peer-reviewed literature as of April 2026. 5. Serving size of 150 g is an estimate based on typical mamak stall portion; no standardised serving size is defined in the HPB source entry.
